rationale: Both correctly diagnose the issue as a run-level misconfiguration (core_model loading dummy weights) matching the thread's 'invalid' resolution, and both cite plausible file/line evidence for the dummy-load-format mechanism and PR #4354. X is more complete: it explains why the full_model pytest marker doesn't itself set the run-level (the actual point of confusion), addresses the A100-vs-H100/MI325 hardware declaration relevant to the reporter's title, checks the video-path prerequisite, and proposes a concrete ergonomics fix (warn/skip when full_model tests run at core_model). Y's core diagnosis is comparably grounded (even includes literal code snippets), but its output is malformed for a maintainer answer — the draft is duplicated verbatim, wrapped in raw internal 'Run report'/'Step diagnostics' scaffolding, and cuts off mid-sentence/mid-command at the end, making it far less usable as a clean end-to-end response.
